samples.xml_description:
Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations. Clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Chip enable signal to initiate multiplication. Multiplicand input. Multiplier input. Product output. Completion flag indicating the end of multiplication. Shift count register, 0 to 16. Assume i is K+1 bits. i bitwise_and

Initialize the shift count register i to 0.

On the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal:

  • Reset i to 0 if the reset signal is active (rst_n == 0).
  • Increment i by 1 if the start signal is active and i is less than 17.

Initialize the multiplicand, multiplier, and product registers to 0.

On the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal:

  • Reset the registers to 0 if the reset signal is active (rst_n == 0).
  • Load the multiplicand (ain) and multiplier (bin) into the registers if the start signal is active and i is 0.
  • Perform the shift and accumulate operation if the start signal is active and i is between 1 and 16:
    • If the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register (areg) is 1, accumulate the shifted multiplier (breg) into the product register (yout_r) by shifting it left by i-1 positions and appending 0 bits at the least significant bit positions.

Assign the product output (yout) the value of the product register (yout_r).
